title: Intermediate Accountant
description: THE OPPORTUNITY Work-model: Hybrid Employment Type : Full Time Reporting to the Lead, Financial Accounting Modernization, the Intermediate Accountant is a critical member of the Accounting group within Shared Services. This role is responsible for accurate and timely recording of financial transactions, maintaining the integrity of the trial balance, and supporting month-end and year-end close processes. The role collaborates closely with internal stakeholders and external partners, including auditors and banks, to ensure compliance, strengthen internal controls, and contribute to process improvements that enhance efficiency and accuracy across the finance function. WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Lead and execute month-end and year-end closing processes, including journal entries, account reconciliations, and account analysis. Support external audits (enterprise-wide and branch level) by preparing working papers and providing required documentation. Assist in statutory and tax compliance, including direct and indirect tax filings and audit preparation. Monitor and record bank transactions; prepare and reconcile period bank statements. Maintain and account for tangible and intangible assets, software implementation assets, prepaids, accounts payable, tax payable, accruals, and other liabilities. Support accounting for corporate credit card transactions and prepare related schedules. Investigate, resolve, and reconcile account discrepancies to ensure accurate financial reporting. Manage relationships with external parties such as banks and auditors. Leverage technology to improve accounting efficiency, strengthen internal controls, and maintain cybersecurity awareness. Contribute to the development and implementation of new processes, internal controls, and streamlined reporting practices. Document and update standard operating procedures, policies, and training materials. Support onboarding, mentoring, and training of new team members, fostering a collaborative and technically proficient team environment. WHO YOU ARE Bachelor ’s degree in Finance , Accounting, or a related field (or equivalent experience). Relevant professional accounting qualification (CPA, CA, ACCA) or enrollment in a CPA program. 3–7 years of relevant experience in similar organizations with exposure to full-cycle accounting. Strong knowledge of accounting principles, internal controls, and financial reporting standards (IFRS, ASNPO, ASPE; GAAP and IPSAS knowledge is an asset). Excellent accuracy, attention to detail, and organizational skills. Ability to meet reporting deadlines and work across time zones (Africa). Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (intermediate–advanced), Teams, BI, Office Connect; Workday ERP experience is a plus. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills; able to present financial information to diverse stakeholders. High integrity, cultural sensitivity, and maturity in a multi-national environment. Results-driven, innovative, intellectually curious, and comfortable with ambiguity. Experience with multi-national accounting, currency, and tax considerations is an asset. Supervisory experience and mentoring skills are a plus. Adaptability to the Foundations values and ability to engage with empathy and purpose. The annual salary for this position is within the range of $ 85,000 to $ 100,000 (CAD) commensurate with experience, skills, and internal equity considerations. ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN RECRUITMENT The Foundation uses artificial intelligence (AI) – enabled tools to screen, assess, and/or select applicants for this position including, but may not be limited to, the use of AI-assisted resume screening and assessments to support the initial stage of the recruitment process. Applications of qualified candidates, as identified by the AI tool, are reviewed by representatives of the Foundation before shortlisting for an interview. ACCOMMODATION The Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. If you require accommodation due to a disability or any other protected ground under applicable legislation during any stage of the recruitment process, please let our recruitment team know by contacting us at: humanresourcesmastercardfdn.org . We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities. Information relating to the need for accommodation and accommodation measures will be addressed confidentially. The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn

title: Program Partner, Canada Programs
description: THE OPPORTUNITY Work-Model: Hybrid Employment Type: Full-Time Reporting to the Lead, Program Delivery, The Program Partner, Canada Programs will play a key role in shaping and advancing strategies that strengthen pathways for Indigenous youth to achieve their aspirations in education and employment. The Program Partner, Canada Programs, will provide expertise and insight to identify opportunities for innovation, collaboration, and capacity building among partners. This role involves supporting the co-creation of programs and partnerships that empower Indigenous youth to address both immediate challenges and systemic barriers within education and the transition to work. The Program Partner, Canada Programs, will combine strategic thinking, partnership management, and a deep understanding of Indigenous contexts to help drive meaningful and sustainable impact. WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Provide expertise and knowledge to support the design and management of programs. Lead internal processes to develop strategies and plans to achieve overarching EleV strategy. Manage current or future programs as appropriate, distilling learnings for Foundation staff. Design and deliver appropriate mechanisms to monitor effectiveness and impact of programming. Identify Indigenous education and employment issues and trends that affect education, economic development, legal and financial systems. Support the development and strengthening of EleV programming in workforce development, entrepreneurship, and economic development. Manage opportunities to strengthen EleV programming through convenings and peer learning networks. Diagnose and contribute expertise and in-depth knowledge of Indigenous education efforts, priorities and programs to identify and leverage innovation and strategic opportunities to build partnerships for sustainable systems change. Expand the Foundation team’s capacity to deliver on major priorities and an expanding network of partners. Engage with other teams on strategic priorities for the Foundation including the digital economy, entrepreneurship, post-secondary education, health, climate, and cultural revitalization. Facilitate the development of strategic partnerships and fulfillment of internal processes to formalize partnerships for systems change. Build relationships with partner communities, organizations, communities of practice and global stakeholders. Engage key public sector officials and other stakeholders on Indigenous education and employment innovation issues. Design and deliver stakeholder engagement sessions and activities. Co-plan and co-facilitate convenings, meetings, and gatherings with partners, youth, WHO YOU ARE Hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in a relevant field such as education, social sciences, management, or public policy, along with significant experience supporting large-scale programs or initiatives focused on Indigenous communities and youth. Bring 4–6 years of program management experience, demonstrating expertise in education, youth leadership, workforce development, and employment programs, ideally with exposure to both the private and non-profit sectors. Have hands-on experience in policy design, implementation, or analysis related to education and transitions to work, particularly in contexts involving First Nations, Inuit, and Métis peoples. Skilled communicator with excellent verbal, written, and presentation abilities, able to engage effectively across diverse cultures and stakeholders. Strong organizational and project management skills, with proven ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced and dynamic environments. Technically proficient in MS Office applications (Word, PowerPoint, Excel) and comfortable leveraging digital tools for coordination, reporting, and presentations. Deeply committed to equity, diversity, and inclusion, with an understanding of and respect for Indigenous ways of being and doing. Professional maturity, sensitivity to different cultures, and impeccable integrity that exemplify the Foundation’s values. Possess excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills with the ability to articulate information to various constituents across cultures. Due to the nature of this position, which requires direct engagement with Indigenous communities, preference will be given to qualified Indigenous candidates. Applicants who wish to qualify for preferential consideration are encouraged to self-identify in their applications. Applicants seeking to qualify for preferential consideration will be required to provide reasonable confirmation of Indigenous identity. Indigenous candidates are encouraged to self-identify and apply even if they do not meet all components listed. You have a commitment to Mastercard Foundation’s values and vision . The annual salary for this position is within the range of $95,000 to $110,000 (CAD), commensurate with experience, skills, and internal equity considerations. ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN RECRUITMENT The Foundation uses artificial intelligence (AI)–enabled tools to screen, assess, and/or select applicants for this position including, but may not be limited to, the use of AI-assisted resume screening and assessments to support the initial stage of the recruitment process. Applications of qualified candidates, as identified by the AI tool, are reviewed by representatives of the Foundation before shortlisting for an interview. ACCOMMODATION The Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. If you require accommodation due to a disability or any other protected ground under applicable legislation during any stage of the recruitment process, please let our recruitment team know by contacting us at: humanresourcesmastercardfdn.org . We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities. Information relating to the need for accommodation and accommodation measures will be addressed confidentially. The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn

title: Lead, Enterprise Risks
description: THE OPPORTUNITY Work Model: Hybrid Employment Type: Full-time Reporting to the Head, Enterprise Risk & Strategic Initiatives, the Lead, Enterprise Risk will work closely with Foundation teams and risk champions to ensure that enterprise risks are systematically identified, assessed, mitigated, and monitored. In this role, you will be responsible for the effective execution and integration of risk management activities across internal Foundation functions, while championing a strong culture of risk awareness throughout the organization. WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Support in coordinating risk identification, assessment, mitigation, and monitoring across all internal functions and strategic initiatives to ensure the ERM program is effective and supports the Foundation’s strategic goals. Collaborate with Foundation teams and risk champions to foster strong risk behaviours, ensuring a shared understanding of the Foundation’s risk appetite, risk tolerance, and risk management processes. Actively participate in risk discussions and provide actionable insights to functional teams. Review and critique risk registers submitted by risk champions and other risk owners, offering insights to improve risk identification, prioritisation, and mitigation strategies. Collect risk information and prepare reports on the enterprise and function-specific risk profile and risk management activities for review by the Head of Risk, Enterprise Risk & Strategic Initiatives. Conduct risk assessments for enterprise-wide initiatives as needed, ensuring that potential risks are identified and mitigated effectively. Plan and conduct regular spot checks and reviews of internal processes to assess compliance with risk management policies. Monitor and analyse emerging risks and trends relevant to the Foundation’s enterprise activities. Support the development and execution of overall resilience strategies, including crisis management and business continuity plans for programs. Drive risk awareness initiatives and training to ensure all levels of staff understand and engage with risk management principles. Support data collection, cleansing, and validation for risk datasets. Perform basic data analysis to identify risk patterns and trends. Maintain accurate documentation and evidence for risk-related activities WHO YOU ARE Bachelor’s degree in risk management, Business Administration, International Development, Public Administration, Economics, Finance, or related disciplines. Professional certifications in Risk Management will be an added advantage. Over 7 years of relevant enterprise risk management experience. Strong understanding of various risk domains ranging from technology, business transformation, finance, and talent. Experience working in culturally diverse environments; proficiency in English is required, and proficiency in French is desired. Strong facilitation and communication skills to convey risk insights across functions. Strong inter-personal skills and ability to engage with empathy and purpose. Cultural awareness and sensitivity, ensuring meaningful engagement and influence across diverse stakeholder groups. Adaptability and learning agility Integrity, courage, sound judgement, and confidence to uphold convictions even in challenging situations. Attention to detail and accuracy Adaptability to the Foundations values and ability to engage with empathy and purpose. T he annual salary for this position is within the range of $125K to $140K (CAD) commensurate with experience, skills, and internal equity considerations. ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE IN RECRUITMENT The Foundation uses artificial intelligence (AI)–enabled tools to screen, assess, and/or select applicants for this position including, but may not be limited to, the use of AI-assisted resume screening and assessments to support the initial stage of the recruitment process. Applications of qualified candidates, as identified by the AI tool, are reviewed by representatives of the Foundation before shortlisting for an interview. ACCOMMODATION The Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to an inclusive recruitment process and workplace. If you require accommodation due to a disability or any other protected ground under applicable legislation during any stage of the recruitment process, please let our recruitment team know by contacting us at: humanresourcesmastercardfdn.org . We encourage candidates to make their accommodation needs known so that we can provide equitable opportunities. Information relating to the need for accommodation and accommodation measures will be addressed confidentially. title: Strategic Advisor - Chief People and Culture Officer (12-month contract)
description: THE OPPORTUNITY Act as the brain trust for the Chief People and Culture Officer (CPCO) on all matters related to people strategy. Take time to understand the work of the Foundation through the senior business partners. Provide strategic advice, insights, and support regarding ongoing enterprise initiatives . Review functional-related plans with the Leadership Team before execution. Collaborate with CPCO to develop or redesign P&C goals and objectives. WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Provide strategic insights and recommendations to support the CPCO in decision- making and executive engagements. Review ongoing strategic plans. Provide recommendations on proposed HR initiatives. Prioritize the P&C roadmap to determine sequencing and pacing. Hold collaborative meetings with P&C Leadership teams. Oversee the consolidation and quality of Board and EXCO presentations. Work with the CPCO on all Board related presentation preparations. P artner with the P&C Leadership Team to prepare EXCO submissions, including consolidation and advisory on strategic positioning. Review and refine strategy documents across the talent value chain to ensure alignment with the Foundation’s mission. Review the P&C modernization strategy with internal teams and the Deloitte Canada working group. Propose overarching goals, objectives , and priorities for 2025–2026. Collaborate with the P&C Leadership Team to develop strategic initiatives. Actively listen to team perspectives and ideas in alignment with organizational values. Ask thought-provoking questions to ensure initiatives are tied to P&C strategy, outcome driven , and measurable. Meet with P&C Leads to provide thought partnership, insights, and guidance to inform strategic choices. Support the P&C Leadership in prioritizing key People and Culture projects and initiatives. Lead conversations with the P &C LT on initiative importance, project impact, resource needs, dependencies, and timing. Monitor progress of strategic projects to ensure alignment with enterprise initiatives. Ensure each strategic project has clearly defined success measures. Define criteria for integrating P&C projects with enterprise initiatives. Gather relevant project information and ensure goal clarity. Suggest success criteria for assessing program outcomes. Critique the People and Culture priorities central dashboard. Review the P&C Dashboard from the reporting team. Analyze critical performance areas, monitor progress, and guide achievement. Plan and coordinate Leadership Team connects and retreats. Develop P&C agendas and manage pre- reads . Capture key action points from meetings. Lead retreat planning and agenda development. Partner with external consultants to ensure delivery of P&C Leadership and extended Leadership retreats. Develop themes, agendas, and session plans. Support the Leadership Team in identifying strategic objectives for sessions and tailoring content to the audience. Design interactive session activities to encourage engagement. Create schedules aligned with the overall strategic plan. Oversee logistical coordination for productive Leadership Team engagements. Project manages P&C Leadership meetings, retreats, and events. Oversee planning activities with the CPCO, Executive Assistant and P&C coordinators. WHO YOU ARE Master’s degree or equivalent. At least 15 years’ experience in a strategic advisory capacity. Experience in People Leadership at an executive level. Executive communication skills. Technical competence in People & Culture systems and processes. Flexible, adaptable, and able to execute a range of job duties and changing priorities. Professional maturity, sensitivity to different cultures , and impeccable integrity that exemplifies the Foundation’s values. Possess excellent verbal, written, and presentation skills with the ability to articulate information to various constituents across cultures. You have committed to Mastercard Foundation’s values and vision . The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn

title: Director Gender
description: THE OPPORTUNITY This role is responsible for building Gender thematic area within Pan-African Programs by setting strategic direction and engaging partners throughout program design and implementation and focused on the Foundation’s commitment to enabling dignified and fulfilling work for young women and young people in general. The role is responsible for providing thematic expertise and facilitating understanding of gender dynamics and supporting establishment of responsive programmatic interventions at all levels of the Foundation WAYS YOU CAN CONTRIBUTE Drive Gender strategy and roadmap development in collaboration with Foundation executive leadership. Work with Foundation leaders and thematic resources to articulate and update the Gender strategy and Roadmap. Provide oversight on Gender programs during the various program lifecycle stages, including scoping, incubation, design, and delivery. Engage and maintain strong strategic relationships with relevant stakeholders, including institutions of interest, peer funders, and others relevant to the Foundation’s work in the field of Gender. Convene thematic expertise and advance thought leadership externally and across the Foundation – at Pan African, E&T, HECIS, and Country Program level. Cultivate relationships and facilitate engagements with thought leaders and other influencers to advance the Foundations thought leadership aligned to Gender. Interface with Foundation business partners across other functional areas to test/refine and deploy thematic area operating models. Lead the team in embedding operational excellence in day-to-day prioritization of work within Gender, team resourcing, delegation of tasks to meet partner demands, performance management input and other people management processes. Interface with Country Directors in the design and delivery of Country Programs to ensure strategic cohesion, coordination of programming efforts, and knowledge sharing. Champion the integration of gender considerations across the Foundation and specifically in Country, Human Capital Development and Pan African Program portfolio design. Lead relevant internal and technical expert teams to advance the Foundation’s overall Gender outcomes in all areas of the Foundation’s work. Collaborate with leadership teams across the Foundation to co-design programs, embed designs, adequate resources and budgets to ensure implementation of safeguarding approaches and mechanisms. Oversee the utilization of the Foundation’s Safeguarding resources and teams to strategically and practically implement policies, procedures and guidelines that meaningfully integrate safeguarding measures across the Foundation. WHO YOU ARE Master’s degree in Social Sciences, including gender/women’s/youth studies, education-related field from a recognized university. Relevant professional qualification. Member of a relevant professional body. Minimum of ten (15) years’ relevant experience in a similar organization setting, with seven (7) years at a senior management level in a Pan African role or Global role. Experience and knowledge of/with the landscape of local, national, regional, and pan-African women’s and youth-serving organizations and networks, as well as knowledge of the country-specific social, economic and political contexts. Experience in partnership management, including capacity building, coordination and networking for effective gender mainstreaming and youth empowerment. Extensive experience in gender and youth and women-centred programme design and implementation in Africa. Experience in leading organizational policy development and delivery on safeguarding and ensuring its effective delivery across a global organization. Experience developing training and facilitating capacity building initiatives on gender, youth and women empowerment and safeguarding. Ability to be flexible within a fast-paced, changing environment Demonstrable influencing skills at a senior leadership level and with high-profile stakeholders. Proven success in developing and leading a global team. Strong interpersonal and influencing skills, able to manage conflict and handle sensitive issues, particularly in cross-cultural and cross-departmental contexts. Positive, solution-focused attitude. Proven people management skills and team-building skills. Experience in the design and analysis of monitoring, evaluation and research. Excellent interpersonal skills and experience working in a multi-cultural team. Excellent written, presentation and communication skills. High attention to detail and the ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines. The Mastercard Foundation values the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the organization and we are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Completion of satisfactory business references and background checks are essential conditions of employment. For more information and to sign up for the Foundation’s newsletter, please visit http://www.mastercardfdn.org/ Follow the Foundation on Twitter at MastercardFdn
